title: Hello and Welcome, I’m Geovanni Flores!
text:
Currently I am a student at Cal Poly Pomona perusing my bachelors degree in business, majoring in marketing. I have gained experience through social media management, and now newly learned data analysis.
Fun fact: I have a passion in traveling and taking photos!

title: Analytics
text:
IBM3202 · Portfolio Website
    Analytics dashboard
    GA4 data  ·  Last 28 days  ·  Tracking: active  ·  Source: Google Analytics 4
  

  
    Key metrics
    
      
        Active users
        39K
        ▲ +12% vs prior period
      
      
        New users
        30K
        ▲ +8% vs prior period
      
      
        Items purchased
        13,230
        ▼ −3% vs prior period
      
      
        Item revenue
        $144K
        ▲ +5% vs prior period
      
    
  

  
    Traffic & engagement
    
      
        Traffic by acquisition channel
        Sessions by source, last 28 days
        
          Organic search
          Direct
          Referral
          Social
        
        
          Sessions by channel: Organic 18K, Direct 12K, Referral 6K, Social 3K.
        
      
      
        New vs returning users
        Engagement comparison, last 28 days
        
          New users · 30K
          Returning · 9K
        
        
          Returning users outperform new users across all three engagement metrics.
        
      
    
  

  
    Purchase funnel — web vs mobile
    
      Funnel drop-off by platform
      Step completion rate starting from session start
      
        
          Web traffic
          Mobile traffic
        
        
          Session start
          
            100%
            100%
          
        
        
          View item
          
            25%
            14.5%
          
        
        
          Add to cart
          
            25%
            19.7%
          
        
        
          Begin checkout
          
            50%
            44.3%
          
        
        
          Purchase
          
            49%
            27%
          
        
      
    
  

  
    Insights & recommendations
    
      
        Retention
        Returning users drive engagement
        Returning users generated more engaged sessions and stronger interaction metrics than new users. Brand familiarity increases both session duration and interaction rate.
      
      
        Drop-off
        Mobile funnel needs optimization
        Mobile users drop off sharply at "View Item" (14.5% vs 25% web). A mobile-first landing page redesign could meaningfully increase conversion rate.
      
      
        Opportunity
        Cart abandonment gap
        45K items added to cart but only 13K purchased — a 71% abandonment rate. Remarketing to cart abandoners within 7 days is the highest-leverage opportunity.
      
      
        Integration
        Google Ads + BigQuery next steps
        Linking Google Ads to GA4 connects ad spend to real conversions. BigQuery export enables multi-session path analysis and custom attribution modeling.
      
    
  

  
    Measurement workflow
    
      Data sourceWebsite / App
      →
      GA4 eventsConversion tracking
      →
      InsightFunnel drop-off
      →
      ActionPage redesign
      →
      Outcome+Conversion rate

title: Analytics
text: This page documents the analytics setup and insights collected from Google Analytics 4 (GA4) for this portfolio website.

title: Analytics
text:
1. GA4 Setup

Account vs Property vs Data Stream
Accounts can be seen as the highest level in Google Analytics — one business can have multiple properties inside of one account. A property is a specific website being tracked; a company can have two websites and one app, each as its own property. A data stream is the source of data inside a property — it is the tracking tag that sends user data into the property.


Key Events Tracked







Event
Purpose




purchase
Conversion event for revenue tracking and ROI analysis


view_promotion
Evaluating marketing effectiveness


add_to_cart
Shows purchase intent; helps analyze drop-off before purchase





Measurement Gaps Identified

Checkout Step Drop-Off Tracking — Without detailed event tracking at each checkout step, identifying where users abandon the process is impossible. Fixing this can increase revenue over time.
Search Tracking Improvements — Internal searches need to be analyzed. Missing this means failing to reveal unmet customer demand.

title: Analytics
text:
2. Traffic & Acquisition

New vs Returning Users
In the last 28 days, there were approximately 39K active users and 30K new users. Returning users showed higher engagement compared to new users — they generated more engaged sessions and had stronger interaction metrics. Comparing to the previous period, engagement levels were stable but returning users consistently outperformed new users in both time on session and interaction depth.

Insight: Familiarity with a brand increases both session duration and interaction rate. Retention strategies should be prioritized alongside acquisition.



Acquisition Channels
Traffic is sourced across organic search, direct, and referral channels. Organic search remains the highest-quality channel due to its intent-driven nature.

title: Analytics
text:
3. Funnel Analysis

Purchase Funnel (Mobile vs Web Traffic)
A funnel exploration was conducted comparing mobile and web traffic across five steps:



Step
Web Completion Rate
Mobile Completion Rate




Session Start
100%
100%


View Item
~25%
~14.5%


Add to Cart
~25%
~19.7%


Begin Checkout
~50%
~44.3%


Purchase
~49%
~27%



Mobile users drop off significantly earlier in the funnel. This suggests the mobile experience may need optimization — particularly at the item view and checkout stages.

Insight: A landing page redesign optimized for mobile could meaningfully increase the mobile conversion rate.

title: Analytics
text:
4. Ecommerce Performance

Top Items by Revenue (Last 28 Days)



Metric
Value




Items Viewed
58,984


Items Added to Cart
45,029


Items Purchased
13,230


Total Item Revenue
$144,723.87



The data shows a significant drop between items added to cart (45K) and items purchased (13K), indicating cart abandonment is a key opportunity area.

title: Analytics
text:
5. Workflow Diagram
The analytics measurement workflow followed in this project:





flowchart LR
  A[Data Source\nWebsite / App Traffic] --> B[GA4 Event &\nConversion Tracking]
  B --> C[Insight: Funnel\nDrop-Off Identified]
  C --> D[Action: Landing\nPage Redesign]
  D --> E[Outcome Metric:\nIncreased Conversion Rate]

title: Analytics
text:
6. Reflections & Recommendations

What I Would Do Differently
I would place greater emphasis on event tracking for key engagement actions — specifically sign-ups, product views, and purchases. Defining conversion events that align with campaign goals allows for more informed marketing decisions, optimizing campaigns based on user actions rather than general traffic metrics.


Recommended Integrations

Google Ads Integration — Connects campaign performance directly to GA4 conversion data, allowing ad spend to be based on actual conversion outcomes rather than clicks.
Audience: Cart Abandoners — Build an audience of users who added items to cart but did not complete purchase within 7 days. Use for remarketing campaigns to increase completed transactions.
BigQuery Export — Enable multi-session path analysis to study user journeys across multiple visits, supporting advanced attribution modeling not available in the GA4 interface.

title: Dashboard
text:
# Marketing Campaign Performance Dashboard - Q1 2026
# BASE R ONLY — no packages required

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# DATA
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────

months  <- c("January", "February", "March")
revenue <- c(54200, 61400, 68000)
spend   <- c(14800, 15900, 17500)

channels     <- c("Google Ads", "Meta Ads", "Email", "SEO")
channel_rev  <- c(74200, 52800, 36400, 20200)
channel_conv <- c(42, 28, 22, 8)
ctr_vals     <- c(24.1, 5.2, 3.8, 2.9)
roas_vals    <- c(9.1, 3.81, 3.5)
roas_ch      <- c("Email", "Google Ads", "Meta Ads")

# Colors matching the original dark dashboard
col_google <- "#4a90d9"
col_meta   <- "#7b6bd6"
col_email  <- "#3dbf8f"
col_seo    <- "#888899"
col_rev    <- "#4a90d9"
col_spend  <- "#7b6bd6"

bg_col     <- "#1a1a2e"
grid_col   <- "#2e2e4a"
text_col   <- "#ccccdd"
title_col  <- "#ffffff"

bar_colors     <- c(col_google, col_meta, col_email, col_seo)
roas_colors    <- c(col_email, col_google, col_meta)

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# SAVE TO PNG
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────

png("marketing_dashboard_Q1_2026.png", width = 1200, height = 1600,
    res = 130, bg = bg_col)

par(mfrow = c(4, 1),
    bg     = bg_col,
    fg     = text_col,
    col.axis = text_col,
    col.lab  = text_col,
    col.main = title_col,
    mar    = c(4, 5, 3, 2),
    oma    = c(1, 1, 3, 1))

# ── Helper: draw dark grid ──────────────────
dark_grid <- function(axis = "y", n = 5, xlim = NULL, ylim = NULL) {
  if (axis == "y") {
    ticks <- pretty(ylim, n = n)
    abline(h = ticks, col = grid_col, lwd = 0.8)
  } else {
    ticks <- pretty(xlim, n = n)
    abline(v = ticks, col = grid_col, lwd = 0.8)
  }
}

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# FIGURE 1 — Monthly Spend vs Revenue
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────

ylim1 <- c(0, 75000)
plot(1:3, revenue,
     type = "n", axes = FALSE,
     xlim = c(0.7, 3.3), ylim = ylim1,
     main = "FIGURE 1 — MONTHLY SPEND VS REVENUE",
     xlab = "", ylab = "")

rect(par("usr")[1], par("usr")[3], par("usr")[2], par("usr")[4],
     col = bg_col, border = NA)

dark_grid("y", ylim = ylim1)

axis(1, at = 1:3, labels = months, col = bg_col, col.ticks = grid_col,
     col.axis = text_col, cex.axis = 0.9)
axis(2, at = pretty(ylim1),
     labels = paste0("$", pretty(ylim1) / 1000, "K"),
     col = bg_col, col.ticks = grid_col,
     col.axis = text_col, las = 1, cex.axis = 0.85)

lines(1:3, revenue, col = col_rev,   lwd = 2.5)
lines(1:3, spend,   col = col_spend, lwd = 2.5)
points(1:3, revenue, col = col_rev,   pch = 19, cex = 1.6)
points(1:3, spend,   col = col_spend, pch = 19, cex = 1.6)

legend("topleft", legend = c("Revenue", "Ad Spend"),
       col = c(col_rev, col_spend), lwd = 2.5, pch = 19,
       bg = bg_col, text.col = text_col, box.col = grid_col, cex = 0.85)

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# FIGURE 2A — Revenue by Channel (horizontal bar)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────

par(mar = c(4, 7, 3, 2))

xlim2 <- c(0, 85000)
bp <- barplot(rev(channel_rev),
              horiz   = TRUE,
              col     = rev(bar_colors),
              border  = NA,
              axes    = FALSE,
              xlim    = xlim2,
              names.arg = rep("", 4),
              main    = "FIGURE 2A — REVENUE BY CHANNEL",
              space   = 0.5)

rect(par("usr")[1], par("usr")[3], par("usr")[2], par("usr")[4],
     col = bg_col, border = NA)

barplot(rev(channel_rev), horiz = TRUE, col = rev(bar_colors),
        border = NA, axes = FALSE, xlim = xlim2,
        names.arg = rep("", 4), space = 0.5, add = TRUE)

dark_grid("x", xlim = xlim2)

axis(1, at = seq(0, 80000, 20000),
     labels = paste0("$", seq(0, 80, 20), "K"),
     col = bg_col, col.ticks = grid_col,
     col.axis = text_col, cex.axis = 0.85)

axis(2, at = bp, labels = rev(channels),
     col = bg_col, col.ticks = bg_col,
     col.axis = text_col, las = 1, cex.axis = 0.9)

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# FIGURE 3 — CTR by Channel (vertical bar)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────

par(mar = c(4, 5, 3, 2))

ylim3 <- c(0, 27)
barplot(ctr_vals,
        col     = bar_colors,
        border  = NA,
        axes    = FALSE,
        ylim    = ylim3,
        names.arg = rep("", 4),
        main    = "FIGURE 3 — CLICK-THROUGH RATE BY CHANNEL",
        space   = 0.5)

rect(par("usr")[1], par("usr")[3], par("usr")[2], par("usr")[4],
     col = bg_col, border = NA)

bp3 <- barplot(ctr_vals, col = bar_colors, border = NA,
               axes = FALSE, ylim = ylim3, space = 0.5, add = TRUE,
               names.arg = rep("", 4))

dark_grid("y", ylim = ylim3)

axis(1, at = bp3, labels = c("Email", "Google", "Meta", "SEO"),
     col = bg_col, col.ticks = grid_col,
     col.axis = text_col, cex.axis = 0.9)
axis(2, at = c(0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25),
     labels = paste0(c(0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25), "%"),
     col = bg_col, col.ticks = grid_col,
     col.axis = text_col, las = 1, cex.axis = 0.85)

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# FIGURE 4 — ROAS by Channel (vertical bar)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────

ylim4 <- c(0, 11)
barplot(roas_vals,
        col     = roas_colors,
        border  = NA,
        axes    = FALSE,
        ylim    = ylim4,
        names.arg = rep("", 3),
        main    = "FIGURE 4 — ROAS BY CHANNEL",
        space   = 0.5)

rect(par("usr")[1], par("usr")[3], par("usr")[2], par("usr")[4],
     col = bg_col, border = NA)

bp4 <- barplot(roas_vals, col = roas_colors, border = NA,
               axes = FALSE, ylim = ylim4, space = 0.5, add = TRUE,
               names.arg = rep("", 3))

dark_grid("y", ylim = ylim4)

axis(1, at = bp4, labels = roas_ch,
     col = bg_col, col.ticks = grid_col,
     col.axis = text_col, cex.axis = 0.9)
axis(2, at = 0:11,
     labels = paste0(0:11, "x"),
     col = bg_col, col.ticks = grid_col,
     col.axis = text_col, las = 1, cex.axis = 0.85)

mtext("Marketing Campaign Performance — Q1 2026",
      outer = TRUE, cex = 1.2, col = title_col, font = 2)

dev.off()

quartz_off_screen 
                2 

message("Saved: marketing_dashboard_Q1_2026.png")

Saved: marketing_dashboard_Q1_2026.png
